_meta.coverage_note: FULL CORPUS — all 30 September dates. September is the first month of the Byzantine ecclesiastical new year (Sep 1 = Indiction / Church New Year). It contains the Nativity of the Theotokos (Sep 8, rank 1 Great Feast) and the Exaltation of the Holy Cross (Sep 14, rank 1 Great Feast). Dates with unconfirmed troparion text carry troparion_status:'text-unavailable'.

commemorations.name: The Indiction — Beginning of the New Ecclesiastical Year; St. Simeon the Stylite

troparion.title: Troparion of the Indiction / Church New Year
troparion.text: O Creator of all things and Controller of the seasons, O Lord, Who alone art without beginning, grant that we may begin this new year with thanksgiving and complete it to Thy glory; graciously accept our supplications, forgiving us our transgressions, through the intercessions of the Theotokos.

commemorations.notes: Indiction — beginning of the Byzantine ecclesiastical year. Also commemorates Simeon the Stylite the Elder (+459). Rank 4.

commemorations.name: Holy Martyr Mamas of Caesarea

troparion.title: Troparion of the Holy Martyr Mamas of Caesarea
troparion.text: Your holy martyr Mamas, O Lord, through his sufferings has received an incorruptible crown from You, our God. For having Your strength, he laid low his adversaries and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

commemorations.notes: Mamas of Caesarea (+275), shepherd-martyr under Aurelian. Rank 4. Standard martyr troparion Tone 4 applied. No unique text confirmed at OCA; standard form used per corpus rules.

commemorations.name: Hieromartyr Anthimos, Bishop of Nicomedia

troparion.title: Troparion of the Hieromartyr Anthimos, Bishop of Nicomedia
troparion.text: Your holy martyr Anthimos, O Lord, through his sufferings has received an incorruptible crown from You, our God. For having Your strength, he laid low his adversaries and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

commemorations.notes: Anthimos of Nicomedia (+302), bishop and martyr under Diocletian. Rank 4. Standard martyr troparion Tone 4 applied. No unique text confirmed at OCA; standard form used per corpus rules.

commemorations.name: Hieromartyr Babylas, Bishop of Antioch, and companions

troparion.title: Troparion of the Hieromartyr Babylas, Bishop of Antioch, and companions
troparion.text: Your holy martyrs Babylas and companions, O Lord, through their sufferings have received incorruptible crowns from You, our God. For having Your strength, they laid low their adversaries and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. Through their intercessions save our souls.

commemorations.notes: Babylas of Antioch (+250) and companions, martyrs under Decius. Rank 4. Standard martyr troparion Tone 4, plural form applied. No unique text confirmed at OCA; standard form used per corpus rules.

commemorations.name: Holy Prophet Zacharias and Righteous Elizabeth, Parents of John the Baptist

troparion.title: Troparion of Sts. Zacharias and Elizabeth
troparion.text: We celebrate the memory of Thy righteous ones Zacharias and Elizabeth, O Lord, and through them we beseech Thee: save our souls.

commemorations.notes: Zacharias and Elizabeth, parents of John the Baptist. Rank 4.

commemorations.name: Miracle of the Archangel Michael at Chonae (Colossae)

troparion.title: Troparion of the Miracle of the Archangel Michael at Chonae
troparion.text: O Commanders of the heavenly hosts, we who are unworthy beseech thee: by thy supplications encompass us with the protection of the wings of thy spiritual glory, guarding us who fall down before thee and fervently cry: Deliver us from all harm, as the commanders of the powers on high.

commemorations.notes: Miracle of the Archangel Michael at Chonae (Colossae), when he defended the sacred spring. Rank 3.

commemorations.name: Forefeast of the Nativity of the Most Holy Theotokos

troparion.title: Troparion of the Forefeast of the Nativity of the Theotokos
troparion.text: From the root of Jesse and from the loins of David, a holy maiden is born today to us; she is the renewal of Adam and the recall of Eve; the source of incorruption, the condemnation of corruption; through her, we are deified, and through her, God is made man. Wherefore let us cry aloud to her: blessed art thou, O Lady Theotokos.

commemorations.notes: Forefeast of the Nativity of the Theotokos. Rank 4.

commemorations.name: The Nativity of Our Most Holy Lady, the Theotokos and Ever-Virgin Mary

troparion.title: Troparion of the Nativity of the Theotokos
troparion.text: Thy nativity, O Theotokos Virgin, has proclaimed joy to the whole universe; for from thee arose the Sun of Righteousness, Christ our God, Who, having abolished the curse, hath given the blessing, and having abolished death, hath granted us life everlasting.

commemorations.notes: Great Feast — Nativity of the Theotokos. Rank 1. One of the Twelve Great Feasts.

commemorations.name: Holy and Righteous Ancestors of God, Joachim and Anna

troparion.title: Troparion of Sts. Joachim and Anna
troparion.text: Joachim and Anna were freed from the reproach of childlessness, and Adam and Eve from the corruption of death, O most holy one, by thy holy nativity. Thy people also celebrate thy nativity, delivered from the guilt of transgressions through thee who gavest birth to God.

commemorations.notes: Synaxis of Joachim and Anna — day after the Nativity of the Theotokos, honoring her parents. Rank 4.

commemorations.name: Holy Martyrs Menodora, Metrodora, and Nymphodora

troparion.title: Troparion of the Holy Martyrs Menodora, Metrodora, and Nymphodora
troparion.text: Your holy martyrs Menodora, Metrodora, and Nymphodora, O Lord, through their sufferings have received incorruptible crowns from You, our God. For having Your strength, they laid low their adversaries and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. Through their intercessions save our souls.

commemorations.notes: Menodora, Metrodora, and Nymphodora (+305), sisters and martyrs. Rank 4. Standard martyr troparion Tone 4, plural feminine group — three solo females treated as group, masculine form applied per corpus rules. No unique text confirmed at OCA; standard form used per corpus rules.

commemorations.name: Venerable Theodora of Alexandria
commemorations.notes: Hold — monastic type. Troparion text not securely confirmed.

commemorations.name: Hieromartyr Autonomus, Bishop

troparion.title: Troparion of the Hieromartyr Autonomus, Bishop
troparion.text: Your holy martyr Autonomus, O Lord, through his sufferings has received an incorruptible crown from You, our God. For having Your strength, he laid low his adversaries and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

commemorations.notes: Autonomus (+4th c.), bishop and martyr in Paphlagonia. Rank 4. Standard martyr troparion Tone 4 applied. No unique text confirmed at OCA; standard form used per corpus rules.

commemorations.name: Forefeast of the Exaltation of the Holy Cross; Consecration of the Church of the Resurrection

troparion.title: Troparion of the Forefeast of the Exaltation
troparion.text: Save, O Lord, Thy people and bless Thine inheritance; grant victories to the Orthodox Christians over their adversaries; and by virtue of Thy Cross, preserve Thy habitation.

commemorations.notes: Forefeast of the Exaltation. Also commemorates the Consecration of the Church of the Resurrection in Jerusalem (335 AD). The Cross troparion is used. Rank 4.

commemorations.name: The Universal Exaltation of the Precious and Life-giving Cross

troparion.title: Troparion of the Exaltation of the Holy Cross
troparion.text: Save, O Lord, Thy people and bless Thine inheritance; grant victories to the Orthodox Christians over their adversaries; and by virtue of Thy Cross, preserve Thy habitation.

commemorations.notes: Great Feast — Exaltation of the Holy Cross. Rank 1. One of the Twelve Great Feasts. A strict fast day.

commemorations.name: Holy Great Martyr Niketas the Goth

troparion.title: Troparion of the Holy Great Martyr Niketas the Goth
troparion.text: Your holy martyr Niketas, O Lord, through his sufferings has received an incorruptible crown from You, our God. For having Your strength, he laid low his adversaries and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

commemorations.notes: Niketas the Goth (+372). Rank 4. Standard martyr troparion Tone 4 applied. Also commemorated Mar 20. No unique text confirmed at OCA; standard form used per corpus rules.

commemorations.name: Holy Great Martyr Euphemia the All-Praised

troparion.title: Troparion of the Holy Great Martyr Euphemia the All-Praised
troparion.text: Thy lamb Euphemia crieth out to Thee with a loud voice: I love Thee, O my Bridegroom, and in seeking Thee I endure suffering, and am crucified and buried with Thee in Thy Baptism; and I suffer for Thy sake, that I may reign with Thee; and I die for Thee, that I may live with Thee. Accept me offered with longing as an unblemished sacrifice. Through her intercessions save our souls, as Thou art merciful.

commemorations.notes: Euphemia the All-Praised — second commemoration (primary: Jul 11). Associated with the confirmation of the Definition of Chalcedon by her relics (451 AD). Rank 3.

commemorations.name: Holy Martyrs Sophia (Faith) and her daughters Vera, Nadezhda, and Lyubov

troparion.title: Troparion of the Holy Martyrs Faith, Hope, Love, and their mother Sophia
troparion.text: Your holy martyrs Faith, Hope, Love, and their mother Sophia, O Lord, through their sufferings have received incorruptible crowns from You, our God. For having Your strength, they laid low their adversaries and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. Through their intercessions save our souls.

commemorations.notes: Faith, Hope, Love (Vera, Nadezhda, Lyubov), and their mother Sophia (+137), martyrs under Hadrian. Rank 4. Standard martyr troparion Tone 4, plural form applied (all-female group treated as group, masculine form per corpus rules). No unique text confirmed at OCA; standard form used per corpus rules.

commemorations.name: St. Eumenios, Bishop of Gortyna

troparion.title: Troparion of St. Eumenios, Bishop of Gortyna
troparion.text: In truth you were revealed to your flock as a rule of faith, an image of humility and a teacher of abstinence. Your humility exalted you; your poverty enriched you. Hierarch Father Eumenios, entreat Christ our God that our souls may be saved.

commemorations.notes: Eumenios of Gortyna (+7th c.), wonder-working bishop of Crete. Rank 4. Standard hierarch troparion Tone 4 applied. No unique text confirmed at OCA; standard form used per corpus rules.

commemorations.name: Holy Martyrs Trophimos, Sabbatios, and Dorymedon

troparion.title: Troparion of the Holy Martyrs Trophimos, Sabbatios, and Dorymedon
troparion.text: Your holy martyrs Trophimos, Sabbatios, and Dorymedon, O Lord, through their sufferings have received incorruptible crowns from You, our God. For having Your strength, they laid low their adversaries and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. Through their intercessions save our souls.

commemorations.notes: Trophimos, Sabbatios, and Dorymedon (+276), martyrs under Probus. Rank 4. Standard martyr troparion Tone 4, plural form applied. No unique text confirmed at OCA; standard form used per corpus rules.

commemorations.name: Holy Great Martyr Eustathios Placidas and his family

troparion.title: Troparion of the Holy Great Martyr Eustathios
troparion.text: Thy martyr Eustathios, O Lord, in his sufferings received an imperishable crown from Thee, our God; for having Thy strength, he laid low his adversaries and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. Through his intercessions, save our souls.

commemorations.notes: Eustathios Placidas (+118), military martyr and his family, martyred under Hadrian. Rank 3.

commemorations.name: Holy Apostle Quadratus

troparion.title: Troparion of the Holy Apostle Quadratus
troparion.text: O Holy Apostle Quadratus, intercede with the merciful God, that He grant our souls forgiveness of offences.

commemorations.notes: Quadratus, one of the Seventy Apostles, 2nd-c. apologist. Rank 4. Standard apostle troparion form (Tone 3).

commemorations.name: Hieromartyr Phocas the Gardener of Sinope

troparion.title: Troparion of the Hieromartyr Phocas the Gardener of Sinope
troparion.text: Your holy martyr Phocas, O Lord, through his sufferings has received an incorruptible crown from You, our God. For having Your strength, he laid low his adversaries and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

commemorations.notes: Phocas the Gardener (+320), martyr at Sinope. Distinct from Phocas the bishop (Jul 23). Rank 4. Standard martyr troparion Tone 4 applied. No unique text confirmed at OCA; standard form used per corpus rules.

commemorations.name: Conception of the Holy Glorious Prophet, Forerunner, and Baptist John

troparion.title: Troparion of the Conception of John the Baptist
troparion.text: Rejoice, barren Elizabeth, thou didst conceive the lamp of the Sun that enlightens all the world and disperses the darkness of ignorance; O ye people, exult for the Forerunner of Christ is born.

commemorations.notes: Conception of John the Baptist by Elizabeth. Rank 4. Six months before the Annunciation (Mar 25).

commemorations.name: Holy Equal-to-the-Apostles Protomartyr Thekla

troparion.title: Troparion of St. Thekla, Equal-to-the-Apostles
troparion.text: Like the Apostles in their ways, and equal to the martyrs in their contests, O Thekla the disciple of Paul, equal to the Apostles, thou didst fill all with wonder by thy godly endeavors; thou didst struggle for the Faith as a first-born among women, enlightening the world by thy glorious feats. O thou great among martyrs and equal to the Apostles, intercede with the merciful God that He may grant us forgiveness of our transgressions.

commemorations.notes: Thekla of Iconium (+1st c.), Protomartyr and Equal-to-the-Apostles, disciple of Paul. Rank 3.

commemorations.name: Venerable Euphrosyne of Alexandria
commemorations.notes: Hold — monastic type. Troparion text not securely confirmed.

commemorations.name: Repose of the Holy Apostle and Evangelist John the Theologian

troparion.title: Troparion of the Holy Apostle and Evangelist John the Theologian
troparion.text: O Apostle, beloved of Christ our God, hasten to deliver thy people without defense; He Who allowed thee to recline on His breast accepts thee as thou dost fall down before Him. Beseech Him, O Theologian, to dispel the persistent cloud of the nations, asking for us peace and great mercy.

commemorations.notes: Primary feast of John the Theologian — his repose (May 8 commemorates a separate miracle). Rank 2 (Polyeleos).

commemorations.name: Holy Martyr Callistratos and companions

troparion.title: Troparion of the Holy Martyr Callistratos and companions
troparion.text: Your holy martyrs Callistratos and companions, O Lord, through their sufferings have received incorruptible crowns from You, our God. For having Your strength, they laid low their adversaries and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. Through their intercessions save our souls.

commemorations.notes: Callistratos and 49 companions (+304), martyrs under Diocletian. Rank 4. Standard martyr troparion Tone 4, plural form applied. No unique text confirmed at OCA; standard form used per corpus rules.

commemorations.name: Venerable Chariton the Confessor
commemorations.notes: Hold — monastic type. Troparion text not securely confirmed.

commemorations.name: Venerable Cyriacus the Anchorite
commemorations.notes: Hold — monastic type. Troparion text not securely confirmed.

commemorations.name: St. Gregory the Illuminator of Armenia

troparion.title: Troparion of St. Gregory the Illuminator of Armenia
troparion.text: By thy illumination of the Armenians, O divinely-blessed Gregory, thou didst drive away the darkness of ignorance; thou didst endure imprisonment without complaint, and wast a luminary to all. O Hieromartyr Gregory, intercede with Christ our God that He may save our souls.

commemorations.notes: Gregory the Illuminator (+ca. 330), Apostle of Armenia. Rank 3.
